skill.name: linear-issues-read
skill.description: Read Linear issues from the command line with filters for team, project, assignee, status, and result limits.
skill.summary: Read and inspect Linear issues through documented CLI commands.
skill.author: Bind

security_audit.summary: Most static findings are Markdown command examples or expected configuration references, not confirmed malicious code. The remaining concern is significant because the skill asks agents to run credentialed Bun commands, but the referenced implementation is not present in the scanned files. No prompt injection or confirmed malicious intent was found, but publication should wait for source review of the actual command scripts.

high_findings.title: Unverified Credentialed CLI Implementation
high_findings.description: The skill declares and documents Bun commands that use Linear issue scripts and a LINEAR_API_KEY, but those script files are not in the scanned file structure. This prevents verification that the commands are read-only and that credentials are handled safely.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced command entry points and required API key are directly visible. The risk is high because the implementation is absent, not because malicious behavior is confirmed.

medium_findings.title: Linear API Key Access Required
medium_findings.description: The static environment access findings are true positives. The skill requires LINEAR_API_KEY from the environment or a git-root .env file, which is legitimate for Linear access but exposes a sensitive credential to any invoked command.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The API key requirement is explicit in both metadata and documentation. The intended use is legitimate, but the credential sensitivity is real.

medium_findings.title: External Bun Commands With User Filters
medium_findings.description: The static command findings are mostly Markdown examples, but they document commands an agent may execute with user-supplied filters. Without the script source, command behavior and argument handling cannot be verified.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands and filter arguments are explicit, but no implementation is available to confirm whether argument handling is safe.

medium_findings.title: Credentialed Network Integration
medium_findings.description: The @linear/sdk dependency and Linear issue-reading purpose indicate outbound access to Linear. The only hardcoded URL in the scanned Markdown is a Bun documentation link, but the skill still depends on networked API behavior.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The SDK dependency strongly indicates network access, while the scanned files do not show the actual request code.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL Finding Is Documentation
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL finding points to the Bun runtime prerequisite link. This is not an exfiltration endpoint or executable network call in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL appears only as a Markdown hyperlink for installing Bun. No code uses it for data transfer.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Finding Is Unsupported
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptography finding at the description line has no supporting evidence in the reviewed file. No cryptographic operation appears in the scanned content.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is a plain description of read-only Linear issue operations. No hashing, encryption, or weak algorithm is present.

low_findings.title: Critical Heuristic Is Not Confirmed Malicious
low_findings.description: The dangerous-combination heuristic is based on documented commands, credential setup, filesystem .env access, and expected Linear network use. No prompt injection, obfuscation, or confirmed exfiltration appears in the scanned files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The risky combination is real at the behavior level, but the reviewed files do not show malicious intent or hidden behavior.

dangerous_patterns.title: Credentialed External Command Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill combines external Bun command execution with access to LINEAR_API_KEY. This pattern requires source review because command code can read and transmit secrets.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The credential and command execution pattern is explicit in documentation. The missing implementation keeps intent unconfirmed.

dangerous_patterns.title: Hidden Environment File Credential Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill instructs use of a git-root .env file for LINEAR_API_KEY. Hidden environment files commonly contain secrets and should be read only by reviewed code.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The .env credential source is stated directly. This is a normal setup pattern, but it increases impact if command code is unsafe.

content.user_title: Read Linear Issues from the CLI
content.value_statement: Teams need quick access to Linear issue details without opening the web app. This skill provides documented CLI commands for listing and reading issues with common filters.
actual_capabilities: Lists Linear issues from a command-line workflow.
Gets a single Linear issue by issue key or UUID.
Filters issue lists by team, project, assignee, and status.
Limits the number of returned issues.
Supports plain terminal output and JSON output options.
limitations: Requires Bun and a valid LINEAR_API_KEY.
The reviewed package does not include the referenced command scripts.
Access is limited by the permissions of the Linear API key.

use_cases.title: Check Active Work
use_cases.description: List current Linear issues for a team and focus on open work.

use_cases.title: Inspect a Ticket
use_cases.description: Open a specific issue by key before writing a summary or implementation plan.

use_cases.title: Prepare Status Updates
use_cases.description: Filter issues by assignee or status to collect progress details.

prompt_templates.title: List Team Issues
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a small team issue list.
prompt_templates.prompt: List up to 10 Linear issues for the Engineering team.

prompt_templates.title: Find Assigned Work
prompt_templates.scenario: Filter issues by assignee and status.
prompt_templates.prompt: Show Linear issues assigned to me that are currently In Progress.

prompt_templates.title: Inspect One Issue
prompt_templates.scenario: Retrieve details for a known Linear issue key.
prompt_templates.prompt: Get the Linear issue ENG-123 and show the important details.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze Filtered Results
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request that combines retrieval with analysis.
prompt_templates.prompt: List Product project issues in Todo status, then summarize blockers and owner patterns.

output_examples.input: List Engineering issues with a limit of 5.
output_examples.output: A terminal issue list showing matching Engineering tickets, their identifiers, statuses, assignees, and titles.
A focused issue view with the issue key, title, status, assignee, project, and available description.

output_examples.input: Show Product project issues in Todo.
output_examples.output: A filtered set of Todo issues from the Product project, ready for review or follow-up analysis.

best_practices: Use a Linear API key with the minimum required permissions.
Review the command script source before publishing or running in production.
Prefer narrow filters and small limits when inspecting issue lists.
anti_patterns: Do not use a highly privileged Linear API key for routine reads.
Do not paste API keys into prompts or command arguments.
Do not assume write safety until the referenced scripts are reviewed.

faq.question: Does this skill modify Linear issues?
faq.answer: The documentation describes read-only list and get commands. The actual script source was not present in the reviewed files.

faq.question: What credentials are required?
faq.answer: It requires LINEAR_API_KEY from the environment or a git-root .env file.

faq.question: Can it filter issue lists?
faq.answer: Yes. The documented list command supports team, project, assignee, status, limit, and JSON options.

faq.question: Can it return JSON output?
faq.answer: Yes. The documented commands include a JSON output option.

faq.question: Why is the audit risk high?
faq.answer: The skill runs credentialed external commands, but the referenced command scripts were not included in the scanned files.

faq.question: Which AI tools are supported?
faq.answer: The report lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.
